zh.lifeSummary: 當Aelbert I Van Egmond於1130年出生時，他的父親Beerwout II van Egmont39歲，母親Rosamunde de Flandre31歲。他和Antoinette de Hainaut至少有2個兒子。他去世於1168年1月22日在荷蘭霍蘭爾享年38歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aelbert I Van Egmond于1130年出生时，他的父亲Beerwout II van Egmont39岁，母亲Rosamunde de Flandre31岁。他和Antoinette de Hainaut至少有2个儿子。他去世于1168年1月22日在荷兰霍蘭爾享年38岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆德。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Egmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Monique, Amie, Andre, Cecile, Desire, Dumas, Jacques, Lucienne, Luckner, Napoleon, Patrice.

Scottish, English, French, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Edmond (Old English Ēadmund, composed of the elements ēad ‘prosperity, fortune’ + mund ‘protection’). It is sometimes found as Admond, where the vowel has been shortened. The personal name was still well used in England in the late 14th century, especially in East Anglia, and was often bestowed in honor of the East Anglian King Saint Edmund the Martyr (died 869), who was killed by pagan Danish invaders.

English: occasionally a variant either of Edman or Eman, the latter from an alternative form of the Middle English female personal name Emeny, arising from Ismaine. The origin of Ismaine is uncertain, though possibly ancient Germanic from an unrecorded Ismagin, perhaps from īs ‘ice’ or īsen- ‘iron’ + -magin ‘strength’, though -magin is not known as a second element. Occasionally Eman may also be a variant of Emond .

variants.name: Edman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Swen, Thor.

English (Lincolnshire): from the Middle English personal name Ed(e)man, occasionally Ad(e)man (Old English Ēadmann, from ēad ‘prosperity’ + mann ‘man’). Alternatively, a variant of Edmond . In Middle English, Edmund (also occasionally found as Admund) was probably sometimes confused with Edman and Adman, and the resulting surnames were almost certainly interchangeable in post-medieval times.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements ed ‘isthmus’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Edmiston
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): habitational name from Edmonstone (Midlothian) or Edmonston (Lanarkshire). The former is so named from the Old English personal name Ēadmund (see Edmond ) + tūn ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Edmonds
meanings: English: variant of Edmond with genitival -s or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Edmondson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Edmond + -son.

variants.name: Edmund
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
English: variant of   Edmond  . This surname is also established in the West Indies.

nameMeanings.name: Aelbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently a modern coinage composed of the name elements Del ( see Delmar , Delroy ) + Bert. In the 1980s it was adopted by the comedian Lenny Henry as the name of one of his comic creations, Delbert Wilkins , and it is used chiefly by people with West Indian connections.

en.lifeSummary: When Aelbert I Van Egmond was born in 1130, his father, Beerwout II van Egmont, was 39 and his mother, Rosamunde de Flandre, was 31. He had at least 2 sons with Antoinette de Hainaut. He died on 22 January 1168, in Schoorl, Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 38, and was buried in Egmond, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aelbert I Van Egmond est né en 1130, son père, Beerwout II van Egmont, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Rosamunde de Flandre, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Antoinette de Hainaut. Il est décédé le 22 janvier 1168, à Schoorl, Holland, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Egmond, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aelbert I Van Egmond 1130 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Beerwout II van Egmont 39 und seine Mutter Rosamunde de Flandre 31. Er hatte mit Antoinette de Hainaut mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 22. Januar 1168 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Schoorl, Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Egmond, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aelbert I Van Egmond è nato il nel 1130, il suo padre, Beerwout II van Egmont, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Rosamunde de Flandre, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Antoinette de Hainaut. Morì il 22 gennaio 1168, nel Schoorl, Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolto a Egmond, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Aelbert I Van Egmondが1130年に生まれた時，父Beerwout II van Egmontは39歳，母Rosamunde de Flandreは31歳であった。彼はAntoinette de Hainautとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1168年1月22日にオランダホーランドスチオオルルで38歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aelbert I Van Egmond(이)가 1130년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Beerwout II van Egmont(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Rosamunde de Flandre(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Antoinette de Hainaut(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1168년 1월 22일 네덜란드 홀랜드 슈를에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aelbert I Van Egmond nasceu em 1130, seu pai, Beerwout II van Egmont, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Rosamunde de Flandre, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Antoinette de Hainaut. Ele faleceu em 22 de janeiro de 1168, em Schoorl, Holland, Países Baixos, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Egmond, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aelbert I Van Egmond родился в 1130 г., его отцу по имени Beerwout II van Egmont было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Rosamunde de Flandre, 31. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Antoinette de Hainaut. Он умер 22 января 1168 г. в местности щурл, холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности эгмонд, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aelbert I Van Egmond nació en 1130, su papá, Beerwout II van Egmont, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Rosamunde de Flandre, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Antoinette de Hainaut. Murió el 22 de enero de 1168, en Schoorl, Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Egmond, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
